context: Tests whether the response correctly identifies the goroutine leak caused by an unbuffered channel send after timeout.

checklist.name: Identifies blocked channel send as the leak
checklist.description: Explains that when the timeout fires, nobody reads from ch, so the goroutine blocks forever on ch <- resp

checklist.name: Suggests buffered channel fix
checklist.description: Recommends making ch a buffered channel (make(chan string, 1)) so the goroutine can send even if nobody reads

checklist.name: Mentions context cancellation as alternative
checklist.description: Suggests using context.WithTimeout and passing it to callExternalAPI so the goroutine itself can abort

checklist.name: No incorrect information
checklist.description: Response does not contain factually wrong statements about Go concurrency, channels, or goroutine lifecycle
